name: Qiskit
description: IBM's open-source quantum SDK for building circuits, simulating systems, and running workloads on real quantum hardware.
whyUseIt: Qiskit is one of the most common entry points into practical quantum programming. It connects conceptual learning directly to simulators and live backends.
fieldPosition: Core circuit design and experiment workflow
engineerWorkflow: Build circuits, transpile them for a target backend, run simulations or hardware jobs, then analyse shot distributions and noise effects.
managerExpects: Code that is reproducible, benchmarked sensibly, and honest about backend limitations.
demoDescription: Create a Bell pair, run it in simulation, then compare the output to a noisy real-device run.
beginnerMistakes: Treating simulator results as if they automatically reflect hardware performance
